description: Business letter from Avraham b. Farrāh, in Alexandria, to Yaʿaqov b. Avraham (and his sons Abū l-Ḥusayn Yūsuf and Abū Saʿīd Ibrāhīm), in Fustat. The writer describes the difficult situation, probably in North Africa, and writes about a merchant who had been lost on the road and almost died before he was found and brought to a ship. He also describes the movement of ships and merchandise in Alexandria and mentions taxes. Dating: June 20, 1054. (Information from Gil)
tags: trade, Alexandria, movement of ships, tax
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מאלכסנדריה. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
